threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when someone asks you to pop to the 'corner shop' instead of the supermarket. The real gems are the tiny local stores—just wait.
Caught two blokes arguing over a seat on the N63 – one insisted it was his 'lucky chair.' Never knew the night buses had such drama.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw that old lady negotiating with the Kebab guy in broken German, both laughing. That's Neukölln, a mix of worlds blending through food.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Neukölln do you find a café where you can sip coffee while browsing for vintage vinyl, surrounded by cats lounging on the record shelves.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know the taco trucks on 16th are a 3 a.m. lifesaver, while newcomers think it’s all about the trendy brunch spots. Totally different vibes.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the 14 Mission crowd with an accordion. Perfect soundtrack to the chaos of a Muni bus that got stuck in a pothole.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss that the quietest streets are the best for real tapas. They stick to the busy spots, but C/Verdi is a hidden gem.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their bewilderment at the lack of escalators in the metro; the locals just glide down the stairs without a second thought.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 149 bus is a reliable way to get anywhere on time. Welcome to our beautiful chaos!
Last night on the 47, a guy serenaded the driver with an off-key rendition of 'Wonderwall'—entire bus was silently vibing. Only in Bermondsey.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a neighbor walk their cat on a leash down Hermannstraße while dodging the flooded potholes. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Neukölln, it’s not uncommon to see a local bring their own plates to a street food stand—no waste and a true community vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their unyielding loyalty to the 14-Mission, even when it’s running late. Tourists schmooze with Uber instead.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a kid with a ukulele belting 'Somewhere Over the Rainbow' on the 14. Everyone singing along. That's Muni magic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the trendy cafes, but the real vibe is hidden in the small plazas where locals gather for a quiet chat.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones staring baffled at the metro map, still trying to understand why it’s called L3 when the line is green.
